new_nodes.title: Death of Lysa Arryn
aliases: Lysa Arryn thrown from the Moon Door
new_nodes.why: THE keystone of the Vale arc — Littlefinger pushes Lysa through the open Moon Door ('Only Cat.' He gave her a short, sharp shove). POV-confirmed; Sansa + Marillion witness; Marillion is then framed for it. Removes Sansa's protector, makes LF Lord Protector outright, spawns the Lords Declarant pact.

new_nodes.title: Sansa adopts the Alayne Stone identity
new_nodes.why: The identity-genesis beat — aboard the Merling King / at the Fingers LF constructs the 'Alayne Stone, my natural daughter' cover, which becomes Sansa's primary self-presentation for the rest of the series. Anchors the cross-identity layer ('Alayne Stone' is already an alias on sansa-stark; alayne-baelish is LF's MOTHER, not this persona).

new_nodes.title: The Lords Declarant confront Littlefinger
aliases: the Lords Declarant pact against Littlefinger
new_nodes.why: The political climax of the AFFC Vale arc — the six Lords Declarant, formed at Runestone after Lysa's fall, climb the Eyrie/Gates to demand LF's removal; Lyn Corbray bares steel; LF negotiates a one-year reprieve. Gives the lords-declarant faction node (0 outbound) its first real edges.

node_alias_hygiene: natural spaced phrases only; no kebab aliases.
dropped_at_synthesis: harry-the-heir-betrothal-plan NODE (Lens A) — the plan's EXECUTION (wedding + Stark reveal) is TWOW/unpublished (forward-dangling); the published-canon betrothal is captured by HB1 (harry BETROTHED_TO sansa) + HB2 (petyr MARRIES_OFF sansa) + W4 (petyr REVEALS_TO sansa). No node for an unexecuted plan.
littlefinger-bribes-nestor-royce-with-gates-of-the-moon NODE (Lens A) — captured as NM2 (petyr MANIPULATES nestor via_bribe) + asserted_relation; over-fragmenting to mint a node.

petyr MANIPULATES sansa (Lens B HB3/K) — MANIPULATES = 'unknowingly used'; by ASOS-06 Sansa is TOLD she is 'a piece' (she knows). Semantic mis-fit + the players/pieces line is better as a ## Quotes attach. LF's agency over her marriage is captured by MARRIES_OFF.
D-2 lysa-accuses-tyrion ENABLES littlefinger-smuggles-sansa (Lens D) — over-distal; the accusation's natural downstream is the AGOT Catelyn-captures-Tyrion chain, not the ASOS smuggle. Drop.
D-4 wedding ENABLES nestor-marillion-death (Lens D) — redundant with SP4 (death-of-lysa CAUSES the Nestor hearing); the wedding->Lord-Protector precondition is folded into SP3.
D-5 murder-of-jon-arryn MOTIVATES lysa-arryn (Lens D) — too interpretive; no 'Vale-stays-neutral' node to anchor the behavioral consequence. Future-node candidate.
D-7 littlefinger-brokers-tyrell-lannister-alliance MOTIVATES petyr-baelish (Lens D) — agency-circular (an event MOTIVATES the person who performed it). Drop.
marillion ASSAULTS sansa-stark + lothor RESCUES sansa (Lens A) — the attempted-assault at the Fingers; deferred to harvest (needs a clean verified quote; ASSAULTS is high-stakes).
sweetsleep / Sweetrobin slow-drugging as a POISONS edge — the murderous intent is a GATED reading (text frames it as 'calming'); captured as ## Quotes / harvest only.
Mya Stone parentage + Mychel/Myranda sub-arcs — parked to harvest / a future Vale pass-2 (kinship likely already in wiki-infobox layer).

edges.quote: So we shall tell Lysa's people that you are my natural daughter
edges.note: The escape from KL is the precondition that necessitates the false identity (Varys's informers make being a Stark unsafe).

edges.quote: The Lady Lysa and I are to be wed
edges.note: DEAD-END FIX: the smuggle (longest-orphaned node) gets its first Vale-ward downstream. Sansa's extraction is the occasion for the long-deferred wedding at the Fingers.

edges.quote: My sweet silly jealous wife
edges.note: BORDERLINE (fresh-verify CAUSES/ENABLES/drop): the instrumental loveless marriage made LF Lord Protector and placed Lysa in his power at the Eyrie, making the Moon Door murder both survivable and worthwhile. Proximate trigger is Lysa's jealous rage; the marriage is the precondition.

edges.quote: He should have followed Lady Lysa out the Moon Door
edges.note: Lysa's death directly occasions the framing hearing — Marillion is condemned for a murder LF committed.

edges.quote: The six had gathered at Runestone after Lysa Arryn's fall, and there made a pact together
edges.note: The Lords Declarant pact forms as a direct response to Lysa's death + LF's consolidation of the Vale.

edges.quote: He gave her a short, sharp shove
edges.note: POV-confirmed dyadic kill (the shove through the open Moon Door).

edges.quote: He gave her a short, sharp shove
edges.note: The executor role on the event hub.

edges.quote: Lysa stumbled backward, her feet slipping on the wet marble. And then she was gone
edges.note: The victim role.

edges.quote: She never screamed. For the longest time there was no sound but the wind
edges.note: Sansa is the POV witness (she had just been dragged to the Moon Door herself).

edges.quote: Marillion gasped
edges.note: Marillion sees the shove — the causal basis for his framing (he is the only other witness).

edges.quote: Sansa crawled from the Moon Door on hands and knees
edges.note: The open Moon Door is the instrument of death — Lysa is shoved backward through it. Lights the moon-door artifact node (0 prior edges).

edges.quote: So we shall tell Lysa's people that you are my natural daughter
edges.note: Sansa enacts the Alayne persona (told the cover en route).

edges.quote: My lady, allow me to present you Alayne Stone
edges.note: LF architects + presents the cover identity.

edges.quote: My lady, allow me to present you Alayne Stone
edges.note: via the Alayne-Stone cover story — LF presents Sansa to Lysa as his bastard daughter.

edges.quote: made a pact together, vowing to defend Lord Robert, the Vale, and one another
edges.note: The faction as collective actor of the confrontation.

edges.quote: spoke of "misrule" that must be ended, and of "false friends and evil counselors"
edges.note: The declaration is aimed at the Lord Protector (LF) — a C2 Petyr-web dyad.

edges.quote: We did not come for your signature. Nor do we mean to bandy words with you, Littlefinger
edges.note: Bronze Yohn, mightiest of the six, leads the parley.

edges.quote: He drew his longsword
edges.note: Corbray bares Lady Forlorn at the parley (the staged near-violence).

edges.quote: Nestor Royce, who hesitated before walking around the table to take the empty chair beside Lord Petyr
edges.note: Nestor sides with LF (bought with the Gates of the Moon).

edges.quote: You told me to put the tears in Jon's wine, and I did. For Robert, and for us!
edges.note: UPGRADE candidate — Lysa names LF as the order-giver of Jon Arryn's poisoning. Proposes RETIRING the S133 `petyr-baelish SUSPECTED_OF murder-of-jon-arryn` (same quote) in finalize IF fresh-verify confirms. Cersei's SUSPECTED_OF (the false misdirection) stays.

edges.quote: And I wrote Catelyn and told her the Lannisters had killed my lord husband, just as you said
edges.note: Sansa overhears the founding crime + the false letter to Catelyn that started the war.

edges.quote: I will wager you that at some point during the evening someone told you that your hair net was crooked and straightened it for you
edges.note: LF discloses the hairnet poisoning mechanism (Olenna lifted the stone) to Sansa.

edges.quote: So those are your gifts from me, my sweet Sansa . . . Harry, the Eyrie, and Winterfell
edges.note: LF reveals the long-game (Harry -> the Eyrie -> reclaim Winterfell).

edges.quote: And I wrote Catelyn and told her the Lannisters had killed my lord husband, just as you said
edges.note: HIGH-VALUE STRUCTURAL FIX: lights the single most important dead-end in the graph — murder-of-jon-arryn had 0 outgoing edges (the root of the whole war).

edges.quote: You are promised to Harrold Hardyng, sweetling
edges.note: The betrothal contract (Alayne Stone, with Lady Waynwood). Sansa's published-canon arc endpoint.

edges.quote: I have made a marriage contract for you
edges.note: LF as the arranger of the Harry betrothal (the political instrument).

edges.quote: With gold and boys and promises, of course
edges.note: The secret paid-enmity arrangement — Corbray feigns implacable hostility to give the Lords Declarant a false opponent. Symmetric covert collusion (NOT MANIPULATES — Corbray is a knowing paid agent).

edges.quote: you and I need only say he lies. Whom do you imagine Lord Nestor will believe?
edges.note: via the staged Marillion confession — LF engineers Nestor's belief that Marillion killed Lysa.

edges.quote: You see the wonders that can be worked with lies and Arbor gold?
edges.note: LF buys Nestor's loyalty with the hereditary grant of the Gates of the Moon (signed as Lord Protector — self-incriminating leverage) + Arbor gold.

edges.quote: I loved her so, I could not bear to see her in another's arms
edges.note: Marillion delivers the coerced false confession (agency compromised, but he speaks the lie to the lords).

edges.quote: when Marillion . . . pushed her
edges.note: Sansa gives the false eyewitness account under duress (her first conscious lie in the game — a key Sansa-character edge).

edges.quote: Ser Lothor Brune stood beside him with a torch
edges.note: LF's agent who executes the river extraction (and the Dontos killing).

edges.quote: Lord Petyr said watch out for you
edges.note: Lothor as Sansa's protector at the Fingers/Eyrie (stops Marillion's assault).

edges.quote: Until your sixteenth name day, I rule the Eyrie
edges.note: Sweetrobin is LF's ward via the Lord Protector regency (formal wardship).
